purpose: Test whether the missing page1 load-event trigger is caused by one unset page mirror callback slot.

basis.target_value_written_meaning: little-endian pointer to page event table start 0x14B

candidates.name: slot_0c_table_start
candidates.diff_summary: 4 callback bytes plus 4 trailing checksum bytes changed
candidates.conclusion: Failed cleanly; page switching stayed healthy but page load did not emit the probe bytes.

candidates.name: slot_10_table_start
candidates.diff_summary: 4 callback bytes plus 4 trailing checksum bytes changed
candidates.conclusion: Failed cleanly; page switching stayed healthy but page load did not emit the probe bytes.

candidates.name: slot_14_table_start
candidates.diff_summary: 4 callback bytes plus 4 trailing checksum bytes changed
candidates.conclusion: Failed cleanly; page switching stayed healthy but page load did not emit the probe bytes.

overall_conclusion: The page1 load-event gap is not solved by writing the page event table start into page mirror callback slots 0x0C, 0x10, or 0x14. The next useful path is an official two-page/page-load oracle or a deeper scheduler descriptor comparison, not more blind slot writes.
safety_notes: All three variants had valid TFT checksums before upload.
All three variants were uploaded to the confirmed TJC8048X543_011C target on COM36.
All three variants preserved page switching and sendme behavior.
